content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `master`, including `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/{model,service}.rs`, and `src/adapters/fs.rs`. Ran targeted tests (`cargo test success_reconciliation`, plus targeted dispatch/reconciliation test commands). Found 1 correctness issue in the milestone replay/idempotency path; no other concrete safety bugs stood out in the changed code.

## Amendments

1. Fix exact-attempt replay handling in the success-reconciliation path. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:278-294` currently encodes the daemon `task_id` into `outcome_detail` (`"task_id=..."`) and then always calls `record_bead_completion`. That is not idempotent when the exact run was already finalized with any different success detail: terminal replays reject mismatched `outcome_detail` values in `src/adapters/fs.rs:3862-3896`, and journal repair rejects the same mismatch in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1048-1083`. I reproduced this locally with an existing succeeded row whose detail was `"first bead completed"`; the replay fails with `task run ... is already finalized with outcome=succeeded`, leaving the bead already closed/synced but the daemon task permanently failing on every retry. Route exact-attempt replays through `repair_task_run_with_disposition` the way `src/cli/run.rs:347-369` already does, or add an explicit task-id parameter to the completion API so legacy success details can be preserved while backfilling linkage.
   Rationale: This breaks the required crash-safe/idempotent recovery path for any bead run whose terminal row already exists with a non-matching success detail, which can wedge a task after the bead mutation has already been committed.
